name: Hormonal Exposures (Endogenous and Exogenous)
definition: Hormonal exposures describe factors that influence lifetime estrogen/progesterone signaling (e.g., age at menarche/menopause, parity, certain therapies). In cancer epidemiology, hormonal context is a population-level modifier that interacts with genetic architecture for hormone-responsive cancers. GENARCH presents this topic in a neutral, educational framing.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Hormone signaling can influence cell proliferation and tissue remodeling in hormone-responsive tissues.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Pubertal timing affects endocrine milieu; population studies often examine lifetime exposure proxies.

references.title: Hormonal factors and breast cancer risk: an overview (review)
